etymology.description: semantic 雨(雨) (yǔ, "rain") + phonetic 彗(彗) (huì, "broom") -> snow
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 雨(雨) (yǔ, "rain") for the semantic idea of precipitation and 彗(彗) (huì, "broom") for its phonetic value. The image of rain light and fluffy as a broom sweep implies the specific type of precipitation: snow.

examples.chinese: 冬天的时候(冬天的時候)，我们经常去山上滑雪(我們經常去山上滑雪)。
examples.pinyin: Dōngtiān de shíhòu, wǒmen jīngcháng qù shānshàng huáxuě.
examples.english: In winter, we often go to the mountains to ski.

examples.chinese: 昨天晚上下了很多雪(昨天晚上下了很多雪)。
examples.english: It snowed a lot last night.

examples.chinese: 雪花在阳光下闪闪发光(雪花在陽光下閃閃發光)。
examples.pinyin: Xuěhuā zài yángguāng xià shǎnshǎn fāguāng.
examples.english: The snowflakes sparkle under the sunlight.
